metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1836
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Presynaptic NPR directly binds to the GluA1-N terminal extracellular domain and stimulates assembly of both excitatory and inhibitory postsynaptic sites. Loss of NPR suppressed surface GluA1 levels, and impaired excitatory synaptic transmission, without affecting synapse numbers.

Fig.2 shows that NPR binds the GluA1-NTD.
Fig.3: NPR, NP1, and NARP binding enhances GluA1 function.. Experimental description: Mouse hippocampal neurons were prepared.

23/6/2017 Pim
-All overexpression constructs are of human species: "The constructs encoding cDNAs of hNPR (HsCD00083063), hNP1 (HsCD00296108), and hNARP (HsCD00040444) were obtained from PlasmID (http://plasmid.med.harvard.edu/PLASMID/)."
- Species of GluA1-NTD overexpressed protein is not mentioned.
